headline: FxFiles Adds Encrypted Collaboration as DePIN Storage Matures
subtitle: Functionland's new secure link-sharing feature arrives as the decentralized storage sector pivots from raw capacity to everyday utility.
briefing: Functionland this week unveiled FxFiles Collaborate, a feature that lets users generate a secure, encrypted sharing link for any folder with a single right-click — bringing cloud-style collaboration to its decentralized storage stack without routing data through a central server. The timing is deliberate. <cite index="4-4,4-5">What started as scattered experiments in storage and wireless coverage has grown into a mature category that now commands serious attention from investors, developers, and everyday users, with DePIN's combined market capitalization sitting in the $9–10 billion range by early 2026.</cite> Within that maturing landscape, <cite index="2-6">storage buyers care about durability, retrieval, and auditability — not narratives.</cite> Functionland's answer is a usability-first move: rather than competing on raw terabytes, it is closing the gap with mainstream tools by making secure collaboration as frictionless as sharing a Google Drive link. The competitive context matters here. <cite index="14-9,14-10">Platforms like Slack and Google Docs were not built for decentralized teams, and most mainstream platforms store sensitive information on servers owned by a small number of corporations.</cite> FxFiles Collaborate addresses that gap directly. Meanwhile, the network now counts 900 active nodes — a supply-side foundation that grows more meaningful as collaboration features draw new users. <cite index="4-30">The sector's shift from hype to paid usage in 2025–2026 shows the model can survive bear markets and still grow.</cite> Watch for how Functionland converts collaboration adoption into measurable node utilization in the weeks ahead.
